function.name: submit_work
function.arguments: {"technology_choices": [{"category": "language", "choice": "Go", "rationale": "Existing codebase is Go; extend rather than introduce a new language"}, {"category": "web_framework", "choice": "net/http standard library", "rationale": "Existing main.go uses net/http; sufficient for adding a single JSON endpoint"}, {"category": "testing", "choice": "net/http/httptest", "rationale": "Standard-library testing harness for HTTP handlers; no external dependencies"}], "component_boundaries": [{"name": "http-service", "responsibility": "Single Go HTTP service exposing / and /goodbye routes via net/http.ServeMux", "dependencies": []}], "data_flow": "Client sends GET /goodbye; main.buildMux dispatches to a handler that writes a JSON {\"message\": \"Goodbye, World!\"} response with Content-Type application/json. No persistence, no external services.", "decisions": [{"id": "ARCH-001", "title": "Extract route registration into buildMux helper", "decision": "Refactor main.go so route registration lives in buildMux() returning *http.ServeMux; main() composes it. This makes the mux unit-testable via httptest without spinning up a real listener.", "rationale": "Without the helper, tests would need to call ListenAndServe; the helper keeps test surface small and fast"}], "actors": [{"name": "HTTP Client", "type": "system", "triggers": ["sends GET request to /goodbye"]}], "integrations": [{"name": "HTTP server", "direction": "inbound", "protocol": "http"}], "test_surface": {"integration_flows": [{"name": "goodbye-endpoint", "components_involved": ["http-service"], "description": "GET /goodbye against buildMux() returns 200, Content-Type application/json, and a body with message=Goodbye, World! — validates handler wiring end-to-end"}], "e2e_flows": [{"actor": "HTTP Client", "steps": ["send GET /goodbye", "read response"], "success_criteria": ["HTTP 200", "Content-Type application/json", "body.message equals 'Goodbye, World!'"]}]}}
